notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
support model counts one beneficiary and active-ability heals/shields only (passive heals, CC, and damage-reduction utility are not scored)
the sim opens on Noxia, which the field rarely builds (1.3% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 0% of the field's core for Maco (it never builds Crescelia, Enra's Blessing, Windcaller) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for heal/shield output: first spike lands around minute 14 (Noxia), third item by minute 29. Take Vesh: +12.9% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.name: Shatterbloom
augments.note: 20 ticks x 15/25/35 (+4% MP each) folded to per-cast totals; assumes full field uptime, one beneficiary

augments.name: Ferocious Flight
augments.note: counted for one enemy hit (single-target convention); own-max-health shield scaling not counted (base health outside item stats)

augments.name: Longshot
augments.note: damage scales with projectile travel distance; positional

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 703 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +60% more one-combo burst: try Noxia over Crescelia (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 3,537 effective HP vs magical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 754 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +49% more one-combo burst: try Noxia over Crescelia (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spirit Of Amir: their burst combo hits 18% less hard, and this kit gives up 18% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spirit Of Amir: their burst combo hits 18% less hard, and this kit gives up 17% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spirit Of Amir: their burst combo hits 18% less hard, and this kit gives up 19% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spirit Of Amir: their burst combo hits 18% less hard, and this kit gives up 16% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Spirit Of Amir: their burst combo hits 31% less hard, and this kit gives up 17% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Mourn's main threat is Abduct; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spirit Of Amir: their burst combo hits 18% less hard, and this kit gives up 19% of its damage for it.

laneSteer.provenance: “Ferocious Flight” ⇒ sustain; the sim models this augment — field support 51.8% over 1,351 games.
